codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested parse, input_tokens, and input_items surfaces, but the parse helper miscomputes structured output for valid responses that contain more than one output-text message/content block.

issues.description: `Responses::parse` deserializes `response.output_text()` instead of parsing individual `output_text` content parts. Because `output_text()` concatenates every message's text, a valid structured response with multiple assistant messages/output_text blocks is turned into invalid JSON and returned as a parse error. The upstream parser walks message/content items separately and returns the first parsed structured output, so this implementation can fail VAL-RESP-013 on multi-message structured responses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `sdk-surface-worker` still hard-requires the brainstorming approval flow for public API changes, but mission workers run in non-interactive exec mode. The worker had to bypass that gate even though the rest of the procedure fit the task, so the skill does not currently match mission reality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/.factory/skills/sdk-surface-worker/SKILL.md` requires brainstorming in its Required Skills and work procedure, while `/Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/beaa247f-5975-4da4-acc2-19deef388841/handoffs/2026-04-05T16-59-50-468Z__responses-parse-input-items-and-token-helpers__7b4e3eed-e34d-4629-a59d-9563d938eb41.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` because exec mode could not pause for the brainstorming hard-gate.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 3d78df0. The input-token and input-item helpers satisfy the contract, but the non-stream parse helper has a correctness bug in how it derives `output_parsed`, so this review fails.
